summary: Ran 27 backend API tests across Auth, Organizations, Users, Alerts, Dashboard, and Multi-tenant Isolation against the external REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All 27 tests passed (100%). Verified JWT + bcrypt auth (bearer + cookies), CRUD flows, role-based access control, and cross-org data isolation. Seed data found working (super admin + client + default org ÑACURUTU SEGURIDAD).

minor.issue: No DELETE endpoint exposed for alerts — test-created alerts accumulate in DB (no cleanup path available). Consider adding DELETE /api/alerts/{id} (super_admin) for maintenance, or a soft-delete flag.
When super_admin passes organization_id query param it filters correctly, but no validation that org exists (returns empty list silently). Low severity.
Uses samesite=none+secure=true cookies which require HTTPS; works in production but may fail in local HTTP dev. Not a bug in current env.

action_items: (Optional) Add DELETE /api/alerts/{id} for super_admin to aid test data cleanup.
(Optional) Fix minor pytest warning: TestAlerts.test_create_silent_alert returns a value — change `return a['id']` to an assert-only pattern.
(Optional) Consider tightening CORS_ORIGINS from '*' in backend/.env for production; '*' combined with allow_credentials=True is ignored by browsers.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: CORS middleware has allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*' — browsers will reject credentialed requests with wildcard origin. Currently works because frontend uses Bearer token in Authorization header, but cookie-based auth from a different origin would fail. Set CORS_ORIGINS to the explicit frontend URL in .env.
server.py: Uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') — should migrate to FastAPI lifespan context manager (non-blocking but warning-worthy).
models.py: AlertCreate allows type silent+normal only (good). Consider validating message length and base64 size limit for image_url/audio_url to avoid abuse of MongoDB document size (16MB cap).

server.py: update_user accepts role changes without checking that an 'admin' is not escalating a user to super_admin. Non-super_admin can set role='admin' via PUT since only create has the check. Recommend mirroring the create_user role-guard in update_user.
server.py: No rate-limiting / brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (system prompt's auth playbook suggests 5-fail lockout). Currently unlimited attempts possible.
server.py: DELETE /api/organizations/{id} does not cascade — orphans users/alerts pointing to the deleted org_id. Consider either blocking delete when users/alerts exist or cascading.

seed.py: Seed creates a default super_admin with organization_id set to the default org. Super admin typically should not be bound to a single org — may cause confusion with multi-tenant queries (though list_orgs handles this correctly).
seed_data_creation: Seed runs automatically on startup via seed_initial_data() — creates default org ÑACURUTU SEGURIDAD, super_admin, and client user. Verified present and passwords valid.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 27 backend tests pass. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_nacurutu_api.py is reusable. Tests do session-scoped login and auto-cleanup of created orgs/users, but alerts remain (no DELETE endpoint). For next iteration, frontend testing and Socket.IO real-time event delivery on alert:new / alert:updated could be covered.
